behaviors.description: Speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure mode is not purely a parsing question — it hinges on whether the service correctly requests speaker diarization from the real STT API and whether the API returns labels in the expected format. REAL-INFRA: An in-process mock always returns whatever speaker-labeled fixture we hand it, so it cannot expose failures like: diarization not requested in the API call parameters, the API silently omitting labels for certain audio formats, or label format changes in the real API response. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) Yes — the failure requires a real HTTP call to the STT API with a real audio payload to verify that speaker labels actually appear in the response. A stub hides this entirely. REJECTION-GATE: The behavior text says 'speech-to-text *output* preserves speaker labels' — 'output' refers to what the real STT API returns, not what our parser does with a pre-fabricated response. No behavior text disqualifies the System level.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a real multi-speaker audio fixture (pre-recorded, ≥2 speakers) to the STT API endpoint with the service's production configuration. Assert the returned transcript segments each carry a non-empty speaker identifier field (e.g., 'speaker_id' or 'speaker_label') and that at least two distinct speaker identifiers appear across the full transcript.

behaviors.description: The structured summary contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure is not in routing or plumbing — it is in whether the LLM correctly identifies task-assignment utterances and attributes them to the right person. LLM-DEP: A deterministic stub would always return whatever action items we hardcode, so it always passes regardless of whether the real model handles attribution correctly. The real failure (LLM misses an assigned task, conflates two attendees, or attributes a task to 'the team' instead of a specific person) only surfaces with a real LLM call. MOCK-HIDE: Mocking the LLM guarantees the test passes by construction; it tests nothing about extraction quality. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (b) Correctness depends on AI/ML output — the model must identify and correctly attribute every assigned task. No stub can reproduce this failure. REJECTION-GATE: There is no behavior text that limits this to a deterministic wiring concern; the word 'assigned' requires semantic understanding of the transcript that only a real model can demonstrate or fail to demonstrate.
behaviors.test_description: Feed a real transcript containing unambiguous task assignments to multiple named attendees (e.g., 'Alice, please draft the proposal' and 'Bob, you will schedule the follow-up call') to the live LLM summarization endpoint. Assert that the returned structured summary's action_items list contains at least one item attributed to Alice and at least one attributed to Bob, with no attendee who received an assignment being absent from the list.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the 'decisions' section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure is semantic classification: does the LLM recognize that 'We have decided to proceed with option B' is a decision and place it in the correct structured field rather than the narrative? LLM-DEP: A stub that always returns a well-formed decisions array trivially passes; the real failure (LLM puts the decision in the narrative, or omits it entirely, or invents a different decision) only appears with a real model call. The structured output schema constraint does not prevent the LLM from leaking content across sections or omitting it. MOCK-HIDE: Any mock that returns correct placement confirms nothing about model behavior. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (b) Correctness depends on real AI/ML classification of sentence intent and correct structured-output placement — both vary across runs and model versions. REJECTION-GATE: 'Not buried in the narrative' is an assertion about model comprehension and output organization, not about API routing or schema validation. No behavior text limits this to a deterministic concern.
behaviors.test_description: Send a real transcript containing one or more explicit decision statements (e.g., 'The team decided to migrate to the new platform in Q3') to the live LLM summarization endpoint. Assert that each decision statement appears verbatim or paraphrased inside the structured output's 'decisions' field, and that the 'narrative' or 'summary' fields do not contain the sole record of that decision.

behaviors.description: When audio is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments, processes them sequentially, and merges the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure is distributed across multiple processing steps: correct splitting boundary detection, independent STT+LLM calls per segment, and coherent merging of multi-segment summaries into one. Each boundary is a real failure surface. LLM-DEP: Each segment generates an LLM summary independently; the merge step itself likely requires an LLM call to produce a 'single coherent' unified output — both are AI/ML-dependent. MULTI-STEP: The pipeline chains at minimum N+1 agentic steps (N segment STT+LLM passes, plus one merge LLM call). Failures in how segment summaries are handed off and unified only emerge when the real sequence runs end-to-end. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (b) Real LLM output governs segment summary quality and merge coherence. (c) Multiple agentic steps must chain correctly. REJECTION-GATE: 'Single coherent summary' and 'merging the results' require evaluating the unified output of a multi-step agentic sequence; no behavior text restricts this to a deterministic wiring check.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a real audio recording exceeding 60 minutes to the service. Verify the service automatically splits it into multiple segments (assert segment count > 1), issues a real STT and LLM summarization call per segment, then issues a merge call. Assert the final unified structured output references topics that appear only in the earliest segment AND topics that appear only in the latest segment, confirming full coverage without gaps or duplicate action items.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline — upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output — completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: By definition this behavior covers the full pipeline, so every module boundary is a potential failure point. REAL-INFRA: Requires real HTTP upload, real STT API call, real LLM call, and real structured output delivery — none of which can be faked without masking end-to-end integration failures. LLM-DEP: The summarization step depends on real LLM output. MULTI-STEP: The pipeline is explicitly a sequence of multiple agentic steps: upload → STT → LLM summarization → structured delivery. This is the canonical Workflow case. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: All three upgrade triggers apply simultaneously: real HTTP infrastructure, real AI/ML output, and multiple chained agentic steps. REJECTION-GATE: 'Full pipeline' and 'without manual intervention' are explicit end-to-end claims; no disqualifying text exists. This is the highest-level, broadest behavior and cannot be demoted.
behaviors.test_description: Upload a real meeting recording (10–30 minutes, multiple speakers, containing at least one decision and one action item assignment) through the service's production intake endpoint. Poll the job status endpoint until the job reaches a terminal state without human input. Assert the final structured JSON payload contains non-empty 'action_items', 'decisions', and 'attendees' fields, that at least one action item names a specific attendee, and that the job reached completion with HTTP 200 (not a timeout or error state).
